title: Publications | Stats NZ

Statistics about the ethnic groups that people identify with
Statistics about the economy measure the goods and services New Zealand consumes and produces, changes in prices, and our economic relationship with the rest of the world
Statistics about the tourism industry in New Zealand
Statistics that count and describe movements of people into and out of New Zealand, and within the country

## Subnational population estimates: At 30 June 2016 (provisional)
This release contains provisional estimates of the resident population of New Zealand's 16 regional council areas (regions), 67 territorial authority areas, and 21 Auckland local board areas.

## New ZealandвЂ™s growth concentrated in handful of regions
The four fastest-growing regional council areas accounted for over 75 percent of New ZealandвЂ™s population growth in the year to June 2016, Statistics New Zealand said today.

## National population projections: 2016(base)вЂ“2068
National population projections indicate the future population usually living in New Zealand, and indicate probable outcomes based on different combinations of fertility, mortality, and migration assumptions.

## Population heads for 5 million
New ZealandвЂ™s population will likely hit 5 million around 2020 and could reach this milestone sooner.

## New Zealand cohort life tables: March 2016 update
Cohort life tables track the mortality experience of people born in each year from 1876.

## Slower labour force growth in the long term
New Zealand's labour force is projected to keep growing, driven by an increasing population and people working into older ages, Statistics New Zealand said today.

## People aged 65+ living in New Zealand
This infographic presents key facts about older people (aged 65+) living in New Zealand in 2013.

## New Zealand as a village of 100 people
This infographic presents an overview of New Zealand's population in 2013.
